extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, whose guard is the conjunction of 8 inputs including seizure_history. rec_drug_b is set true only by r4, whose guard is seizure_history alone. Each output has exactly one writer rule, so priorities/ties are irrelevant. Whenever r1 fires (rec_drug_a=true), seizure_history must be true, which necessarily fires r4 (rec_drug_b=true). Therefore the conjunction r1-guard implies both outputs true. Setting all 8 conjuncts of r1 to true (seizure_history, anticoagulated, asthma, g6pd_deficiency, elderly, diabetes, pregnancy, qt_prolongation = true; renal_impairment and prior_gi_bleed arbitrary, here false) yields rec_drug_a=true AND rec_drug_b=true, violating the mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b).
